skill.name: ckm-brand
skill.description: Brand identity support for voice, visual style, messaging, asset organization, brand guideline updates, and design token syncing.
skill.summary: Create and maintain brand guidelines, voice rules, visual identity, and asset standards.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical hits, but most are false positives from markdown examples, color hex values, CSS snippets, and documentation links. The confirmed issues are medium-risk local script behaviors: filesystem reads and writes, prompt-context generation from user-controlled brand files, and one shell-based token generation command.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution for Token Generation
medium_findings.description: The sync script imports child_process and calls execSync to run a local design-token generator. The command is intended and uses fixed project paths, but shell execution can become risky if the working directory or generator path is controlled by an attacker.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The child_process import and execSync call are direct evidence. Confidence is not higher because the command target is a fixed local helper and no network or credential use was found.

medium_findings.title: Project File Reads and Writes
medium_findings.description: The scripts read brand guidelines, read and write design-token files, inspect assets, and read a local asset manifest. This is expected for the skill, but users should run it only in repositories where those file changes are acceptable.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are explicit and aligned with the skill purpose. The risk is operational rather than malicious because paths are local brand and asset files.

medium_findings.title: Brand Context Can Carry Prompt Instructions
medium_findings.description: The context injection script reads brand guideline content and emits it into a prompt-like brand context. If the source brand file is untrusted, malicious guideline text could influence later AI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script intentionally creates prompt context from local markdown. I found no embedded attack text, but the design can propagate untrusted instructions if users point it at hostile brand files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Color and CSS Values
low_findings.description: The repeated weak-cryptography findings map to hex colors, CSS snippets, and design-token examples rather than cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or credential protection logic was found in the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain brand color hex values and CSS examples. This explains the pattern hits and does not indicate cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Are Not Runtime Network Calls
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links to font and color resources in markdown or HTML examples. They are documentation references and do not execute network requests from the skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in reference material and template snippets. I found no fetch, curl, wget, or HTTP client behavior in the executable scripts.

low_findings.title: C2 and Reconnaissance Keywords Are Benign Documentation Terms
low_findings.description: The C2 and reconnaissance heuristics are triggered by ordinary brand workflow words such as sync, control, command, and key. The reviewed context describes design-token syncing and brand instructions, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is brand documentation and local verification commands. I found no persistence, beaconing, remote endpoint, or credential collection intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: execSync Uses a Shell Command String
dangerous_patterns.description: The token sync script builds a shell command string for execSync. Fixed constants reduce the risk, but array-based process execution would be safer for paths containing spaces or shell metacharacters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is directly present. The confidence is moderated because it points to a local script path and output file, not obvious attacker-controlled input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Writes to Design Token Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The sync script writes updated design tokens to disk and may regenerate CSS. This can modify repository files when run, so users should review diffs after execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operation is explicit. It is expected behavior, but it is still a meaningful security and integrity consideration for marketplace users.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when voice rules, colors, typography, and assets live in separate places. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for brand systems, asset checks, and design token updates.
actual_capabilities: Provides reference frameworks for brand voice, messaging, visual identity, typography, color, logo usage, and approvals.
Generates brand context from a local markdown guideline file for use in AI-assisted content and design work.
Validates local marketing asset names, formats, file sizes, and manifest registration.
Extracts brand color palettes from markdown and gives instructions for comparing image colors.
Syncs brand color choices from guidelines into design token JSON and CSS files when the expected project files exist.
Includes a starter brand guidelines template for new or incomplete brand systems.
limitations: It does not verify legal trademark ownership or licensing rights.
Image color extraction is helper-based and may require ImageMagick or another visual analysis tool.
Token syncing expects specific file paths and may not match every design system structure.
Brand context output depends on the quality and safety of the local guideline file.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Brand System
use_cases.description: Create an initial voice, color, typography, messaging, and asset structure from the included starter template.

use_cases.title: Review Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Check asset naming, size, format, manifest registration, logo rules, and color usage before a campaign ships.

use_cases.title: Keep Tokens Aligned
use_cases.description: Update brand colors in guidelines and sync matching values into local design token files.

prompt_templates.title: Create Starter Guidelines
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new brand
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ckm-brand skill to create starter brand guidelines for a product named [PRODUCT]. Include voice, colors, typography, logo usage, messaging, and asset rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review Brand Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Check an existing page or campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ckm-brand references to review this content for brand consistency. Focus on voice, messaging clarity, color usage, typography, and asset naming.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Asset Approval
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand to evaluate assets in [ASSET_PATH]. Report naming issues, file format concerns, size warnings, manifest status, and approval checklist gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Brand Into Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ckm-brand to update brand colors in docs/brand-guidelines.md, run a dry review of token changes, and explain the expected updates before syncing files.

output_examples.input: Review a landing page draft against our brand voice.
output_examples.output: A concise audit listing voice mismatches, unclear claims, missing proof points, and rewrite suggestions grouped by page section.

output_examples.input: Create guidelines for a professional analytics brand.
output_examples.output: A structured brand guide with voice traits, color palette, typography rules, logo usage, messaging pillars, and asset approval rules.

output_examples.input: Check a campaign asset before release.
output_examples.output: A validation summary with pass or fail status, filename issues, size warnings, supported format notes, and manifest registration guidance.

best_practices: Keep docs/brand-guidelines.md reviewed and approved before using it as AI prompt context.
Run token syncing in a clean working tree and review file diffs before committing changes.
Pair automated asset checks with human review for trademark, accessibility, and campaign fit.
anti_patterns: Do not point the context injection script at untrusted brand files without reviewing the text first.
Do not treat generated guidelines as legal approval for logo, trademark, or licensing use.
Do not run token syncing blindly in repositories with unrelated design token structures.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full brand guide?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a starter template and references for voice, messaging, colors, typography, logo usage, assets, and approval checks.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Will it change files automatically?
faq.answer: Some scripts can read and write local brand and design-token files when you run them. Review diffs after execution.

faq.question: Does it fetch data from the internet?
faq.answer: No runtime network calls were found in the scripts. Some reference documents include external font and color resource links.

faq.question: Can it validate image colors directly?
faq.answer: It extracts palette data and provides comparison helpers. Full image analysis may require ImageMagick or another visual tool.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive brands?
faq.answer: Use trusted local guideline files and review generated prompt context before sharing it with external AI tools.
